skipped.name: SSRN
skipped.reason: LISTING BUG FIXED 2026-07-08 but a HARDER block remains underneath. Fixed: index_url now points at the ERN (Economics Research Network) category page, which lists real papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=NNNNNNN links directly (no 2-level crawl needed) — added article_query_re since abstract_id lives in the query string, not the path, so the generic path-based detector could never match it. Confirmed via a direct collect_source() test: 55 real abstract_id candidates found (vs 15 static category links before), 0 dropped_old. BUT: papers.ssrn.com itself serves a Cloudflare interactive challenge ('Just a moment...') on every single abstract page, tested with a completely FRESH browser context (not the ARK-style context-flagging pattern — this fails even cold, first visit, no prior navigation) and with waits up to 20s+. This is a materially harder block than ARK's context-based flag; the fresh_context_per_article fix that recovered ARK does NOT help here. Worth a one-off test once/if a residential IP is available (uncertain whether IP reputation is even the driver here vs an interactive JS/proof-of-work challenge), but not assumed to be residential-recoverable.
